commit.message:
feat: add AQ-50 and AQ-10 tests, fix ASRS 18-item integration

- Add AQ-50 (Baron-Cohen et al., 2001): 50 items, binary scoring, 5 subscales (SOC/ATT/DET/COM/IMG), cutoff ≥32
- Add AQ-10 (Allison et al., 2012): 10 items, binary scoring, cutoff ≥6
- Create AQResults.jsx and AQ10Results.jsx components with subscale charts and item breakdowns
- Fix ASRS_SUBSCALES import in App.jsx (was missing, caused runtime reference error)
- Fix PatientProfile maxScores: asrs 24→18, add aq:50, aq10:10
- Add aq/aq10 to TEST_META, getTestStatus, and history color coding
- Fix single-quote escape in aq.js Czech question

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v2: Fix scraper extracting platform-level links instead of individual job URLs

Problem: v1's generic extractor grabbed navigation links ("产品与技术"),
category labels ("技术运营类"), and JD text fragments as job entries.
ByteDance returned 0 real jobs, Tencent had no URLs, MiniMax had 30+
duplicate fragments.

Fix:
- Add platform-specific extractors for Feishu, ByteDance, Tencent
- URL pattern filtering: only keep /position/{ID}, /job_detail/{ID} etc
- Click-through for SPA cards that use click handlers instead of <a> tags
- Auto-pagination (default 3 pages)
- Proper deduplication by URL or title
- Structured field extraction (title, location, department, jobType)

New difficulty section added to README (Difficulty #8).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add step-based consolidation: no episode boundaries needed

consolidation_interval=N consolidates every N Hebbian steps when reward
EMA is positive. Combined with delta_decay, gives continuous learning:
fast weights decay naturally, good patterns accumulate in slow memory.

commit.message:
Simplify render.ts: extract modules, delete dead code, replace workarounds

Split render.ts (1961 → 1048 lines) into focused modules:
- views/explorer/sidebar-render.ts — file tree sidebar (~510 lines)
- views/tabs/tab-bar.ts — tab management (~370 lines)
- views/status-bar/status-bar.ts — status bar (~185 lines)

Delete ~700 lines of dead explorer code (file-tree.ts, file-tree-item.ts,
file-operations.ts, index.ts) and their test file — never imported at runtime.

Replace exp0-exp15 (16 individual variables) with Set<number> for directory
expansion tracking, removing the 16-directory limit. Replace strEq() with
native === (Perry string comparison now fixed).

PDF白紙問題を修正: margin配列エラーを解決

Restructure archilume into subpackages (core, geo, post, apps, infra, workflows)

Reorganize flat module layout into a proper package hierarchy:
- archilume/core/: rendering pipeline, sky/view generation, octree, materials, MTL converter
- archilume/geo/: IFC/OBJ geometry tools (inspector, cleaner, slicer)
- archilume/post/: HDR/TIFF post-processing and animation
- archilume/apps/: interactive editors (matplotlib + Dash)
- archilume/infra/: GCP VM manager and Accelerad scripts
- archilume/workflows/: high-level workflow orchestration

Also add GEMINI.md, ROADMAP.md, docs/radiance_scratchpad.md, and examples/DOCKER_GCP_TRANSITION_PLAN.md.

feat: page convertisseur Airtable → Grist

Page statique client-side pour migrer les données EMILE d'Airtable vers Grist.
- Onglet 1 : Accompagnants (6 champs directs + Date_ajout_Airtable)
- Onglet 2 : Candidats (80+ champs, logique Permis/Besoin_divers/Difficultes)
- convert.ts : parser CSV, sérialiser CSV, conversions de types
  - parseEuropeanDate : DD/MM/YYYY HH:MM → ISO 8601
  - toChoiceList : booléens Airtable → ["L",...] Grist
  - Permis : 6 colonnes Airtable → ChoiceList + Permis_statut
  - Besoin_divers, Difficultes_diverses : agrégation multi-colonnes
- Aperçu 5 lignes + téléchargement CSV avec BOM UTF-8

* docs: add specification and implementation plan for website i18n audit tool

Add complete feature specification (012-website-i18n-docs) for a Go-based
CLI tool to audit translation coverage of GoZen website documentation.

Specification includes:
- 3 prioritized user stories (P1: identify missing, P2: translate, P3: maintain)
- Technical research and technology decisions (Go 1.21+, Cobra, Lipgloss)
- Data model with core entities (DocumentationPage, Translation, Locale, AuditReport)
- CLI interface contracts (audit, sync-check, list-missing commands)
- Quickstart guide with 7 usage scenarios
- Complete task breakdown (57 tasks across 6 phases)

Current translation coverage baseline:
- zh-Hans/zh-Hant/es: 56.3% (9/16 files)
- ja/ko: 0% (0/16 files)
- Overall: 33.8%

Missing 7 v3.0 files: agent-infrastructure.md, bot.md, compression.md,
health-monitoring.md, load-balancing.md, middleware.md, usage-tracking.md,
webhooks.md

Tool will be implemented as standalone binary in website/tools/i18n-audit/
with future option to integrate as 'zen docs audit' subcommand.

Implement User Story 1 (P1): Identify Missing Translations

- Reporter: Generate formatted table reports with lipgloss
- CLI: audit command with --docs-path, --i18n-path, --locale flags

Implementation details:
- Go 1.21+ with Cobra CLI framework
- Dependencies: goldmark, yaml.v3, lipgloss

Implement User Story 2 (P2): Translation Workflow Support

New functionality:
- list-missing command: List missing translations for a specific locale
- Priority sorting: Sort missing files by size (largest first)
- Path suggestions: Show exact file paths where translations should be created

Implementation details:
- PrioritizeMissingFiles(): Sort by file size descending
- GenerateMissingReport(): Generate formatted report with suggested paths
- CategorizePriority(): Categorize files into P1/P2/P3 tiers

* feat: add sync-check command for outdated translation detection (User Story 3)

Implement User Story 3 (P3): Maintain Translation Consistency

New functionality:
- sync-check command: Detect outdated translations by comparing modification times
- Git integration: Use git log for reliable timestamp comparison
- Staleness detection: Flag translations older than source files
- Exit code behavior: Exit 1 if outdated translations found, 0 if all up-to-date

Implementation details:
- DetectOutdatedTranslations(): Filter translations where source is newer
- GetGitModTime(): Execute git log -1 --format=%ct for file timestamps
- GenerateSyncReport(): Generate formatted table with source vs translation dates
- OutdatedFile type: Track file path and modification times
- Fallback to file system mtime if git fails

Tests added:
- TestDetectOutdatedTranslations: Verify staleness detection logic
- TestGenerateSyncReport: Verify report formatting and content

CLI flags:
- --docs-path: Source documentation directory
- --i18n-path: Translation directory
- --locale: Check specific locale only (optional)

Verified against real website:
- All current translations are up-to-date
- Exit code 0 when no outdated files detected
- Git-based timestamps more reliable than file system mtime

Tasks completed: T035-T041 (User Story 3)

* fix: resolve flaky TestDaemonSurvivesCLITermination test

Fix two issues causing test failures:

1. PID parsing: Update regex to match actual output format
   - Was looking for "PID:" but output is "(PID 12345)"
   - Now correctly extracts PID from parentheses format

2. Temp directory cleanup: Fix permission denied errors
   - Go module cache files are read-only by default
   - Add cleanup handler to chmod files before TempDir removal
   - Prevents "permission denied" errors during test cleanup

This resolves the flaky test that was failing on both main branch
and PR branches.

commit.message:
fix: remove unnecessary 7zip-bin and app-builder-bin from root deps

These were added by previous commits but are transitive dependencies of
electron-builder and do not need to be in the root package.json. Their
presence as direct root deps corrupted the npm cache for app-builder-lib,
causing NSIS templates (messages.yml) to go missing on CI and failing the
Windows build.

Removing them changes the lock file, which invalidates the cached npm
packages on GitHub Actions and forces a clean install on the next run.

commit.message:
Fix product breakdown modal to use new 365-day forecast system

The product breakdown modal was querying the deprecated dashboard_salesforecast
table which had no data, resulting in "No product forecasts available" errors.
Updated to use the new SalesForecastBase system with 365-day base forecasts.

Changes to dashboard/views.py:
- Updated forecast_product_breakdown() to query dashboard_salesforecastbase
- Changed from horizon parameter to start_date/end_date parameters
- Use daily_forecasts JSON field instead of forecast_data
- Filter 365-day forecasts to requested date range
- Return num_days in response for dynamic display

Changes to dashboard/templates/dashboard/sales_forecasting.html:
- Updated viewProducts() JavaScript function
- Pass start_date and end_date from current filter form
- Display dynamic date range in modal header (e.g., "90 days")
- Show correct total days in table header

Root Cause:
System migrated from old SalesForecast (horizon-based) to new SalesForecastBase
(365-day base) but product modal endpoint was never updated. Old system had
no product-level forecasts for most schools.

Result:
Product breakdown now correctly shows all products for a school filtered to
the currently selected date range. For Western Heights High: now shows ~77
products instead of "No products available".

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(catalog): implement product detail page and reviews per spec-04

Backend:
- GET /api/v1/products/:slug — full product detail with variants, related, ratings
- GET /api/v1/products/:slug/reviews — paginated reviews
- POST /api/v1/products/:id/reviews — submit review (ParseUUIDPipe, rate-limited 5/hr)
- New DTOs: ProductDetailDto, ProductVariantDto, ReviewDto, CreateReviewDto, PaginationDto

Database:
- Add Review model to Prisma schema with product/user relations
- Migration: 20260306000000_add_reviews
- Add client_backend generator to keep backend node_modules in sync

Frontend:
- /products/[slug] SSG page with generateStaticParams + generateMetadata
- JSON-LD Product and BreadcrumbList schemas (XSS-safe </script> escaping)
- Components: VariantSelector, AddToCartSection, ReviewsList, ReviewForm,
  RelatedProducts, StarRating, Breadcrumbs
- loading.tsx and error.tsx boundaries

Tests:
- 21 new unit tests for findBySlug, getReviews, createReview, findAllSlugs

commit.message:
feat: update lab Alloy config from production config.alloy

Port the production pipeline structure into the Docker lab config:
- discovery.relabel for eventsproc with hostname instance label
- Full filter_and_format processing stage (log_type, event_source,
  activity, level extracted; log_type + activity as structured metadata)
- splunk_filter stage drops non-application events before Splunk
- otelcol sending_queue added (num_consumers=10, queue_size=1000)
- Loki URL: http://loki:3100/loki/api/v1/push  (no mTLS)
- Splunk URL: http://splunk:8088/services/collector/event  (no mTLS)
- Prometheus: localhost placeholder (Grafana scrapes eventsproc directly)

Log source stays as loki.source.docker — eventsproc runs as a container
in the lab, not a systemd service, so journald is not available.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: netbird-events
repository.description: This Golang application fetches Netbird event logs and writes them directly to standard output. It is designed to sit upstream of collectors like Grafana Alloy or OpenTelemetry, allowing you to forward logs to any supported backend (Loki, Splunk, ELK, etc.) without proprietary dependencies.

commit.message:
Fix: use validation_alias for env var binding

pydantic-settings v2 uses validation_alias (not alias) for env var
lookup. alias=DB_HOST made it look for env var HOST, not DB_HOST.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
